channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-04
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects, with notable updates in the scienceverse/metacheck and JamieCummins/regcheck repositories. In metacheck, several branches were actively developed, including fixes to the LLM and plumber endpoints, as well as the introduction of a bibr-JSON REST API and transparent resource caps. These updates demonstrate the interconnected nature of our projects, with metacheck's advancements in data management and validation likely to benefit other tools, such as regcheck. Speaking of regcheck, a recent update removed the sign-up feature, streamlining the user experience. Overall, our community's collaborative efforts are driving innovation and improvement across the board, and we celebrate the hard work and dedication of our contributors. As our projects continue to evolve, we look forward to exploring the exciting possibilities that emerge from these connections and advancements.

74 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

docpluck31 commits
regcheck18 commits
metacheck15 commits
escicheck6 commits
statcheck2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 11 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

Work was done on the following branches: `jw_llm_fixes`, `jw_plumber_bibr_api`, `jw_bibr_read_passthrough`, `jw_grobid_text_fix`, `jw_schema_sync_10_5`, `jw_fable_fixes`, and `data_check`. The `jw_llm_fixes` branch saw a fix to correct the "ollama-native gate" using "&& not %%" and added "fail-fast param/model checks". On the `jw_plumber_bibr_api` branch, a feature was added for a "bibr-JSON REST API" with "validated uploads, HTML report, hardened runtime". The `jw_fable_fixes` branch had multiple commits, including fixes to the "code_check green path", "ref_miscitation report", and "traffic lights", as well as changes to the "plumber" endpoint pipeline and the addition of a "/modules endpoint". The `data_check` branch introduced "Transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, data-manifest + archive tooling", as described by Lakens. Other branches, such as `jw_grobid_text_fix` and `jw_schema_sync_10_5`, addressed specific issues, including stopping "anchor normalization from corrupting paper text" and syncing the "bundled paper.json to bibr v10.5", respectively.

11 commit(s) today:

  • a73d52a [jw_llm_fixes] fix(llm): correct ollama-native gate (&& not %%); fail-fast param/model checks — Jakub Werner
  • d99956e [jw_plumber_bibr_api] feat(plumber): bibr-JSON REST API — validated uploads, HTML report, hardened runtime — Jakub Werner
  • 4daee8c [jw_bibr_read_passthrough] feat(read): let bibr abstract and figure/table captions flow through — Jakub Werner
  • 00d98b8 [jw_grobid_text_fix] fix(grobid): stop anchor normalization from corrupting paper text — Jakub Werner
  • 4d53313 [jw_schema_sync_10_5] fix(schema): sync bundled paper.json to bibr v10.5 — Jakub Werner
  • fdc5a1d [jw_fable_fixes] tests: fix test_that wrapper warnings, add expect_disjoint, env-aware .onLoad test — Jakub Werner
  • 994fb58 [jw_fable_fixes] modules: fix code_check green path, ref_miscitation report, traffic lights, stale imports — Jakub Werner
  • de6fd04 [jw_fable_fixes] core: fix GROBID text corruption, LLM crashes, API hardening — Jakub Werner
  • d91dc7e [jw_fable_fixes] read: let bibr abstract and figure/table captions flow through .read_bibr — Jakub Werner
  • 9483c49 [jw_fable_fixes] plumber: DRY endpoint pipeline, /modules endpoint, report=false, hardened Docker runtime — Jakub Werner
  • c42ecbf [data_check] Transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, data-manifest + archive tooling — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: statcheck: activity update
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed from the same branch of the `https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck` repository. The merge commit on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was made to integrate changes from the remote repository. Additionally, on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, "admin updates" were made, as per the commit message. No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The changes were limited to the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, with no other branches or modules mentioned.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 12 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a commit addressed the issue of "chan_feldman T3 caption-tail-walk TEXT-LOSS" by adding a diagnostic and an xfail guard, with the fix being deferred as it requires corpus-wide changes. Another commit on the 'main' branch updated the documentation to record the outcome of "run-3" and resolved "W0l ×-as-3 residuals", also rescoping "efendic headings to B6" as part of the documentation triage. A fix was applied to the 'glyph' module on the 'main' branch to recover two residual "×-as-3 prose shapes" that "W0k can't reach", related to "efendic" and version "v2.4.116". Additionally, a rendering fix was made on the 'main' branch to remove an orphaned affiliation remnant in the Abstract body, related to "chandrashekar" and version "v2.4.115".

12 commit(s) today:

  • ba4e124 [main] docs+test(rc-t): diagnose chan_feldman T3 caption-tail-walk TEXT-LOSS; xfail guard (fix is corpus-wide, deferred) — Gilad Feldman
  • 56f60b5 [main] docs(triage): record run-3 outcome — W0l ×-as-3 residuals resolved (v2.4.116); efendic headings rescoped to B6 — Gilad Feldman
  • a4f8796 [main] fix(glyph): recover 2 residual ×-as-3 prose shapes W0k can't reach (efendic) + v2.4.116 — Gilad Feldman
  • e140cf1 [main] fix(render): strip affiliation remnant orphaned in Abstract body (chandrashekar) + v2.4.115 — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
